name: Panama

colors.symbolism: peace and the purity of national life

description: The flag is divided into four equal rectangles: upper-hoist white containing a blue five-pointed star, upper-fly red, lower-hoist blue, and lower-fly white containing a red five-pointed star.
history: Designed by María de la Ossa de Amador, wife of the first Panamanian president, in 1903 following Panama's separation from Colombia on 3 November 1903. The flag was first hoisted on 3 November 1903 and provisionally adopted on 4 June 1904 by Law 64; it was confirmed as the national flag by the National Assembly on 4 June 1904 and constitutionally fixed on 15 December 1949. The colours deliberately reference the United States flag — to acknowledge the role of the U.S. in Panamanian independence — while reorganising them to symbolise national reconciliation between the two political parties.

features.symbolism: the quartered field represents the political compromise at independence — the white quarters for peace shared by both parties, with the blue (Conservative) and red (Liberal) quarters acknowledging the two principal political movements of the new republic
the authority and law that uphold the two virtues
